titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Sage Scanlon-Perez in the Center for Primary Research Training (CFPRT), under the supervision of Emily Wittenberg, 2026.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 May 14.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-05-29 11:03:24 -0700.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

did.abstract:
Irina Kosmovska was born in Moscow, circa 1914. During the Russian Revolution Kosmovska was smuggled out of Russia as an infant and spent her early childhood in Berlin and Paris. It was there that she began her early ballet training with Victor Gsovsky. That foundation would lead her to continue learning and dancing with many of the great dancers and teachers of the time including the Maryinsky ballerinas Olga Preobrazhenska and Lubov Egorova.
Kosmovska was sponsored to come to the U.S. in 1951, as part of a Congressional bill that permitted certain European artists special entry to the United States. Upon arriving in Los Angeles she began teaching in various ballet schools and choreographing. In 1959, she founded the Los Angeles Junior Ballet, which existed through 1985, and became the official school of John Clifford's Los Angeles Ballet. Additionally, in the early 1960s, Kosmovska taught for George Balanchine's School of the American Ballet in New York, and with American Ballet Theatre (ABT) students in Los Angeles and in Saratoga in the summers. She was recognized as "a revered expert on instruction in the pirouette."
The collection consists of photographs, correspondence, manuscripts, programs, scrapbooks, press coverage and programs documenting the life and work of Irina Kosmovska as a dancer and later as the founder of the Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company. The collection also documents the personal life of Kosmovska including personal notes and extensive family photographs.
Correspondence documents internal operations of the Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company, individual student participation, and financial components. Press coverage spans Kosmovska's career as a ballerina to her success in choreography and leading the Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Irina Kosmovska Collection (Collection 2358). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by Sage Scanlon-Perez under the supervision of Emily Wittenberg, 2026

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Irina Kosmovska was born in Moscow, circa 1914. During the Russian Revolution Kosmovska was smuggled out of Russia as an infant and spent her early childhood in Berlin and Paris. It was there that she began her early ballet training with Victor Gsovsky. That foundation would lead her to continue learning and dancing with many of the great dancers and teachers of the time including the Maryinsky ballerinas Olga Preobrazhenska and Lubov Egorova.

She debuted in 1936 with Col. de Basil's Ballet Russe de Monte Carlo, then partnered with Paul Goube in Barcelona, where she also began to teach and choreograph ballet works. The Second World War broke her connection with that company. Kosmovska was sponsored by impresario Sol Hurok to come to the U.S. in 1951, as part of a Congressional bill that permitted certain European artists special entry to the United States. Upon arriving in Los Angeles she taught for Marge and Gower Champion and spent twelve consecutive summers teaching at the School of American Ballet, the official school of the New York City Ballet.

In 1959, she founded the Los Angeles Junior Ballet, which existed through 1985, and became the official school of John Clifford's Los Angeles Ballet. Additionally, in the early 1960s, Kosmovska taught for George Balanchine's School of the American Ballet in New York, and with American Ballet Theatre (ABT) students in Los Angeles and in Saratoga in the summers. She was recognized as "a revered expert on instruction in the pirouette."

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of photographs, correspondence, manuscripts, programs, scrapbooks, press coverage and programs documenting the life and work of Irina Kosmovska as a dancer and later as the founder of the Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company. The collection also documents the personal life of Kosmovska including personal notes and extensive family photographs.

Correspondence documents internal operations of the Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company, individual student participation, and financial components. Press coverage spans Kosmovska's career as a ballerina to her success in choreography and leading the Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p:
This collection has been arranged in the following series:
Series 1: General Records (1964-1983)
Series 2: Irina Kosmovska Records (1958-1993)
Series 3: Los Angeles Junior Ballet Records (1956-1991)
Series 4: Photographs (1919-1983)
Series 5: School of American Ballet Records (1934-1979)

controlaccess.subject: Ballet -- Study and teaching -- California -- Los Angeles
Ballet dancers -- Photographs
Ballet dancers -- California -- Los Angeles -- Archives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents various ballet and dance companies recitals and performances not specifically created by Kosmovska. These companies existed within the same time period and showcase dance accomplishments at the time.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files within the series are arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes The Ballet Theatre Annual, Royal Danish Ballet, and Sadler's Wells Theatre Ballet magazines.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the Los Angeles FM and Fine Arts Magazine and two copies of Performing Arts by the Dorothy Chandler Pavilion.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a complete catalog of piano music by G. Schirmer and a Collection of Classical Ballet Variations.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Collection of Classical Ballet Variations includes loose notes and correspondence maintained in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the personal writings of Kosmovska as in notes, choreography, children's stories. Includes some press coverage documenting her career, and personal notes and holiday cards from family and students.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files within series arranged alphabetically, some individual files maintain original order others are arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies of the Western Ballet Association, inc. invitations to its first Ballet Ball 'Paris Comes to Los Angeles.' A gala dinner and divertissements at the Beverly Hilton Hotel. Includes pages dedicated to the choreographer, Kosmovska.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes funeral memory book for Kosmovska's father, Michael Popoff. As well as copies of the funeral program for Irina Kosmovska.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Items arranged chronologically. Includes one bound booklet maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clippings featuring Kosmovska.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes holiday carts, thank you notes and other personal cards.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically with undated materials at the back of the folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten financial ledger, choreography, games, speeches, and drawings

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains handwritten notes and some drawings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original bound order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thesis investigating Kosmovska's teaching methodology of pirouettes, includes her biography, definition of pirouettes, and her former students.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order.

did.unittitle: The Adventures of Little Rhino Bound Draft

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collection of short stories for children writen by Kosmovska in draft form with some copy edits. Includes a letter from 2007 with co-author Jennifer Lee Daniels who attempteds to publish posthumously.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original bound order.

did.unittitle: The Adventures of Little Rhino Draft

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collection of short stories for children writen by Kosmovska. Includes a handwritten, type writer, copy, and typed edition. The typewriter edition includes a note from Kosmovska, "any alterations or imitations are subject to my approval."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original order.

did.unittitle: Series 3: Los Angeles Junior Ballet Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the founding and successes of the Los Angeles Junior Ballet company through correspondence, event flyers, press coverage, and season brochures.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged alphabetically, indvidiaul folders reflect original order when discernible or were arranged chronologically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Associated Dancing Teachers of Souther California certification for Kosmovska, and a resolution recognizing Los Angeles Junior Ballet.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes several flyers for Los Angeles 'JR' Ballet season performances.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pamphlet about the Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company, schedule of classes.

did.unittitle: Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company Season Brochures

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company Season Brochures

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a file maintained in original order with original label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper clippings and magazines accompanied with notes highlighting the press coverage.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents Kosmovska's success as a dancer, choreographer, and teacher. Includes family photographs with great representation of Heidi Popoff, Kosmovska's mother. Documents the recitals and practices of the Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Photographs were originally separated from the rest of materials and organized thematically, that that original order was maintained throughout the series. Photo albums and scrapbooks were disassembled due to conservation needs with original order maintained. Folders within the series are arranged alphabetically.

did.unittitle: Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of practices, performances, and staged shots of individual dancers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Three envelopes of photographs that were rehoused but maintained in original order.

did.unittitle: Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of practices, recitals, staged studio shots of dancers, and performance photos.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained within an original envelope in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in an original envelope in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Includes an original envelope of photographs maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Kosmovska's mother, Heidi Popoff. A majority of the portraits were taken in a studio in Paris.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portraits of Kosmovska's mother, Heidi Popoff. Majority of the portraits are of Popoff later in life.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company by Andre Le Gagnoux

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Studio portraits of dancers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of the Popoff family depicting different outings, holidays, and family events. Some photographs contain aditiona context and metadata written on the back.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a photo album disassembled and individually wrapped in mylar for preservation purposes. Maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed portraits of dancers addressed to Kosmovska.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portraits ranging from Kosmovska's performing days to her time leading the Los Angeles Junior Ballet Company. Mostly portraits with a few full body photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Kosmovska dancing and press coverage.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of an album disassembled with pages individually wrapped in mylar for conservation purposes. Maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings of press coverage, portraits, photographs of Kosmovska dancing, dance programs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a disassembled scrapbook, individual pages are individually wrapped in mylar. Contents are maintained in original order. Halfway through the scrapbook there were loose clippings that have been rehoused into an envelope maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the recitals, events, and internal correspondence of the American School of Ballet.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series was arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochures detailing the schools services for students, faculty, curriculum/courses, rates, and other operational information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters between Kosmovska and the exectuive assistant of the School of American Ballet, Nathalie Gleboff as well as with Eugenie Ouroussow.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.
